title: Mildly Fermented Portuguese Spaghetti Pasta
description: A traditional Portuguese one-pot dish featuring spaghetti, mother-sauce-base, and a touch of fermentation, made without added sugars.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the canned tomatoes, tomato paste, fermented vegetable broth, salt, black pepper, and dried basil, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Add the dried spaghetti to the pot, stirring to combine with the sauce.
Continue to simmer, covered, for an additional 15 minutes, or until the spaghetti is al dente.
Remove the pot from the heat and let it cool slightly before serving.
